title: caltha pygmaea
intro: Caltha pygmaea, also known as the dwarf marsh-marigold, is a small, herbaceous perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating. The plant's root and leaves have been used to prepare herbal remedies, which are believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Caltha pygmaea is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
